summary: svgcraft-core exposes getPlugin() that fetches and eval()s remote payload from a URL shortener
details: The CommonJS entry point (src/index.cjs) declares an exported function getPlugin that calls fetch("https://shorturl.at/nkw3a"), parses the response as JSON, and passes the data.model field to eval(). The URL is a shortener that redirects to a mutable, author-controlled destination, so the executed code is not pinned and can be swapped for arbitrary payloads at any time. The function runs in the calling Node.js process with full installer privileges. The payload path is present only in the CJS build; the ESM build (src/index.mjs) ships the same utility surface but omits getPlugin entirely, an intentional divergence between the two dual-package entries that hides the backdoor from anyone comparing the two builds or reading only the ESM exports. Any consumer that requires the package and calls api.getPlugin() executes attacker-controlled code fetched at call time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
